name: Rayon (Data Parallelism Library for Rust)

performance.notes: Work-stealing thread pool; scales to all CPU cores automatically

agent_readiness.notes: Data parallelism with .par_iter() drop-in replacement for .iter(). use rayon::prelude::*. vec.par_iter().map(|x| expensive(x)).collect(). par_iter_mut(), into_par_iter(). Parallel sort: par_sort(). rayon::join for parallel tasks. ThreadPoolBuilder for custom pools. Work-stealing scheduler automatically balances load. Zero data races guaranteed by Rust's type system. Used by ripgrep, cargo, rustc. For CPU-bound parallel computation — use Tokio for async I/O.

gotchas: Not for async I/O (use Tokio for that)
Parallel overhead not worth it for small collections (<1000 items)
Thread-local state doesn't work across par_iter
best_when: CPU-bound parallel data processing (map, filter, reduce over large collections)
Image processing, data transformation, scientific computing in Rust
Any CPU-bound Rust code that needs to use all cores
avoid_when: Small collections where parallel overhead exceeds benefit
Tasks with heavy shared mutable state
summary: Rayon provides data parallelism for Rust via .par_iter() — a drop-in replacement for .iter() that runs on a work-stealing thread pool. use rayon::prelude::*; then vec.par_iter().map().collect(). Automatic load balancing across all CPU cores. Zero data races (Rust type system). Used by ripgrep, cargo. For CPU-bound parallel computation. Use Tokio for async I/O parallelism.
